revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23986660476|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:日吉神社 (神戸町)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Hiyoshi Shrine | location = {{ill|Gōdo, Gifu|en|Gōdo, Gifu|qid=Q1203575|lt=Gōdo, Gifu|1=Gōdo, Gifu}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|qid=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35.4252222|N|136.6043528|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|en|Oyamakui no Kami|qid=Q10935105|qid=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}
{{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Kami|ja|大貴己神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579021|1=Ōkuninushi}} | Festival = May 3rd - May 4th | Founded = 817 (Kōnin 8) | Former Ranking = Former '''Prefectural Shrine''' (県社), {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q119929592}} | image = File:Hiyoshi-jinjya Godo03.JPG | location = {{ill|Godo|qid=Q1203575|1=Gōdo, Gifu}} }} [[File:Hiyoshi-jinjya Godo04.JPG|thumb|right|220px|Three-story Pagoda]] '''Hiyoshi Shrine''' (日吉神社) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Gōdo, Anpachi District, Gifu Prefecture, Japan'''. It was historically classified as a '''prefectural shrine (県社)''' and '''Kinpei-sha (金幣社)''' under the '''modern shrine Ranking system (近代社格制度)'''. == History == According to shrine tradition, '''Hiyoshi Shrine was founded in 817 (Kōnin 8) by Saichō (最澄)''', the founder of '''Tendai Buddhism'''. The '''Enryaku-ji temple''', which controlled the '''Hirano Manor (平野荘)''', enshrined '''Hiyoshi Taisha (日吉大社) from Ōtsu, Sakamoto''' as the guardian shrine of this estate, similar to other estates they administered. As a result, it became the '''protective shrine of the manor'''. Two temples, '''Zen’in-in (善学院) and Kangaku-in (勧学院)''', were established by '''Anpachi Tayū Anji (安八太夫安次)''' as the shrine’s '''shrine-temples (別当寺)'''. Following the '''Meiji Restoration''', the shrine underwent '''Shinbutsu Bunri (神仏分離, separation of Shinto and Buddhism).''' == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * '''Three-story Pagoda (三重塔)'''{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kenzobutsu/goudo/hiyosimie.html |title=Hiyoshi Shrine Three-story Pagoda |publisher={{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|qid=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}} |accessdate=2013-04-19}} ** Built during the '''late Muromachi period (1467-1572)''' ** Structure: '''Three-bay, three-story pagoda''', '''hinoki bark roof (檜皮葺)''' ** '''Designated as an Important Cultural Property on April 17, 1914 (Taishō 3)''' * '''Wooden Seated Jizō Bosatsu Statue (木造地蔵菩薩坐像)''' – Known as the '''"Omokaru Jizō" (おもかる地蔵)'''{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tyoukoku/tyoukoku.html |title=Carvings (Sculptures) |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-25}} * '''Two Wooden Seated Eleven-faced Kannon Statues (木造十一面観音坐像 2躯)''' * '''Stone-carved Guardian Lion-dog (狛犬, Komainu) Pair''' === Gifu Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Honden (Main Hall) with Togiwabumi (棟札, Ridge Beams Inscription)'''{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kenzobutsu/goudo/hiyosihonn.html |title=Hiyoshi Shrine Honden |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-19}}{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.godo.gifu.jp/content/areaguide/event/juyo05.htm |title=Important Cultural Properties (Architecture & History) |publisher=Gōdo Town |accessdate=2013-04-25}} === Gifu Prefecture Designated Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property === * '''Mikoshi (Portable Shrine) and 108 Lantern Stands (神輿及び百八燈明台)'''{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/yuumin/goudochou/hiyosijinn.html |title=Hiyoshi Shrine Mikoshi and 108 Lantern Stands |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-19}} === Gifu Prefecture Designated Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * '''Gōdo Sannō Matsuri (神戸山王まつり, Gōdo Sannō Festival)'''{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/goudochou/goudosannnou.html |title=Gōdo Sannō Matsuri |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-05-14}} == Access == * '''5-minute walk''' from '''Hirogōdo Station (広神戸駅)''' on the {{ill|Yōrō Railway Yōrō Line|en|Yōrō Railway Yōrō Line|qid=Q8062665|lt=Yōrō Line|1=Yōrō Railway Yōrō Line}}, operated by '''Yōrō Railway (養老鉄道)'''. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.town.godo.gifu.jp/content/areaguide/event/juyo.htm Gōdo Town Cultural Heritage Information] * {{Commonscat-inline}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|日吉神社 (神戸町)|version=101190222|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:日吉神社 (神戸町)]] {{Commons category|Hiyoshi-jinjya (Godo)}} {{wikidata link|Q11505069}} [[Category:Architecture of Kōbe Town]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Cultural_heritage_monuments_in_Japan]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Gōdo, Gifu]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Gifu prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kōbe]] [[Category:Prefecture-designated Tangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Sannō Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with 50+ untranslated japanese characters]]
